titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the M. Esther Harding Collection
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday November 6, 2020 at 04:56

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Chiefly in English; some writings in German.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from the C. G. Jung Institute of New York on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2012.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The M. Esther Harding Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: M. Esther Harding Collection. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

bioghist.head: M. Esther Harding
bioghist.p: M. Esther Harding (1888-1971) was an American psychoanalyst. She was early adherent to the theories of C. G. Jung and the first significant practitioner of Jungian psychoanalysis in the United States.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into eight series: I. Jungian Seminar Records, 1923-1937. II. Analytical Notes and Journals, 1920-1970. III. Writings, 1922-1970. IV. Correspondence and Criticism, 1946-1969. V. Writings of Others, 1971. VI. Photographs, 1909-1968. VII. Artwork. VIII. Printed Material.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections [as they are acquired], and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically by date of the seminar documented.

did.unittitle: "Dreams of Peter Blobbs" Seminar, September 1920, Sennen Cove, Land's End, Cornwall, England
M. Esther Harding seminar dinner speech
Human Relationships Seminar, July 14-26, 1923, Polzeath, England (The Cornwall Seminar)
Manuscript and typescript notes by M. Esther Harding, Kristine Mann, and Cary de Angelo
Fabric and board wrapper for Cornwall Seminar notes
Analytic Psychology Seminar, March 23-June 6, 1925, Zürich, Switzerland
Dreams and Symbolism Seminar, July 25-August 7, 1925, Swanage, England (The Swanage Seminar)
Manuscript and typescript notes by Harding and possibly Eleanor Bertine

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscript notes of conversations with Jung, possibly by Eleanor Bertine, not specific to any seminars.

did.unittitle: Manuscript and typescript notes by Harding
Psychological Analysis of Nietzsche's Zarathustra Seminar, June 3, 1936, Zürich, Switzerland
"Answer to a Question concerning the Archaic Elements in the Self"
Manuscript notes, possibly by Eleanor Bertine
"Some Aspects of Personality from the Point of View of the Psychoanalyst," given before the Cosmopolitan Club, London
"She:" A Portrait of the Anima. A Critical Analysis of the Novel by Sir Henry Rider Haggard, first read in the Seminar by C.G. Jung, Kusnacht-Zurich
"Psychoanalyzing The Captive", in the new Ever magazine
"A Short Survey of Dr. Jung's Article – Redemption Ideas in Alchemy"
Typscript of a book review, " Two Essays on Analytical Psychology by C.G. Jung," which appeard in The Nation , December 12, 1928
Typescript of a book review, " Contributions to Analytical Psychology by C.G. Jung," which appeared in The New York Times Book Review , May 12, 1929

Typescript of a book review, "'The Ordinary Man and the Unconscious' review of Two Essays on Analytical Psychology by C.G. Jung / translated by H.G. and C.F. Baynes," possibly published in The Dial , 1929
Clipping of a book review, " Jung's Two Essays on Analytic Psychology and Contributions to Analytic Psychology ," which appeared in The New Republic on February 6, 1929
Clipping of a book review, " Gestalt Psychology's Challenge to the Behaviorists by Wolfgang Kohler," which appeared in The New York Times Book Review , May 19, 1929
"Christmas Message from the Point of View of Analytical Psychology," in Concern, Vol. 12, no. 10, December 1970, p. 15-20, published by United Presbyterian Women.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Texts that include criticism of Harding's writings and correspondence relating to publishing, including letters from Random House, Bollingen Foundation, Indian Psycho-Therapeutical Society, and Paul Mellon.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes program for a memorial meeting, October 22, 1971, at the United Engineering Center, New York, given by the Psychology Club of New York City, NYAAP, and C.G. Jung Foundation for Analytical Psychology, and remarks by William Kennedy at Harding's funeral, May 15, 1971.

did.unittitle: "M. Esther Harding, Bailey Island, Maine, August 5, 1968"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inscribed "To Esther Harding on the occasion of her 80th birthday with love and esteem from her friends and colleagues."

did.unittitle: "Dr. Harding, Dr. Bertine, Dr. Mann," Vol. 13
Album of portraits of C. G. Jung
Photographs of Esther Harding, C.G. Jung, etc.

descriptivenote.p: Photographs of Esther Harding, C.G. Jung, etc.

did.unittitle: Photographs of Esther Harding, C.G. Jung, etc.
Oil painting of C. G. Jung
The Life and Work of St. Paul by Frederic W. Farrar

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Shrewsbury High School prize "Old Girls" presented by Archdeacon Maude to Mary Esther Harding.
